mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0% or the signal loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain throughput, which supports near-term equity price appreciation over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 65% of high-inflation months or inflation fails to predict return direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market weakness, suppressing corporate profit margins and generating negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion, which validates equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns during established expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals fail to predict positive returns in greater than 60% of expansion quarters or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below the 95.0 index threshold signals severe supply-side contraction and inventory depletion, which triggers short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances when INDPRO is below 95.0 or the threshold loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.
